title: Thunderbird on Android Mobil Phone
creator: sabine.haluszka-seidel
content:
I'm not happy with the way the mailbox folders are displayed on my mobile phone.

When using numerous subfolders, it is very confusing compared to the display on the PC.


Example:

INBOX.00.AAA


INBOX.01.AAA.aaa

INBOX.01.AAA.bbb

INBOX.01.AAA.zzz


INBOX.02.AAA.aaa

INBOX.02.AAA.bbb

INBOX.02.AAA.zzz


INBOX.99.AAA.aaa

INBOX.99.AAA.bbb

INBOX.99.AAA.zzz


This drives me to despair on the small screen of my mobile phone.


IT WOULD BE BETTER IF THESE HIERARCHY LEVELS WERE REPRESENTED BY INDENTATIONS, AS IS USUALLY THE CASE!


Have I overlooked something, are there settings for this somewhere?


Sabine Haluszka-Seidel

sabine.haluszka-seidel@posteo.de

title: Thunderbird Android no longer sending or receiving
creator: firefox1898
content:
Hello, Mi 9 SE with MIUI 12.5.1 Android 11 RKQ1.200826.002 Thunderbird 10.0

I moved from K9 to Thunderbird some time ago without incident. In the last few days I realised I had received nothing and checked on my PC and indeed mails were waiting. I tried to send a test mail from the phone, the mail remained in the OUTBOX and I received the following error message :- "Failed to send some messages ErrnoException: connect failed: EACCES (permission denied)" I checked and this is an Android error and various articles I found that suggest necessary changes to the manifest which I am not capable to change; I am not a coder. I decided to save the configuration, delete and reinstall Thunderbird. After this and reapplying the passwords the waiting mails were delivered. I then tried to send a test message from the phone and again the same error was generated with the phone blocked again from sending or receiving.

I suspect that there was an upgrade applied with a changed manifest that broke the operation of Thunderbird with Android 11.

Can someone please verify this, it may be impacting many installations on older phones?

Many thanks!

title: How to configure for multiple IMAP path namespaces
creator: Terry.Ingoldsby
content:
The IMAP server I use has two namespaces that I access using Thunderbird desktop. The first namespace is "/" - effectively "INBOX". The second is a public namespace shared by numerous persons. Let's call it "Public" for purposes of discussion.

Thunderbird desktop correctly identifies that I have access to both namespaces and I can see folders in each. On Thunderbird Android (beta), if I "Auto-detect IMAP namespace" it correctly finds "/" (i.e., my private INBOX) but does not see the "Public" folders. If I turn off "Auto-detect IMAP namespace" and enter the IMAP path prefix explicitly (i.e., "Public") it can then see the Public folders, but not my private INBOX.

The workaround has been to create two accounts in T-Bird Android, one for INBOX and the other for Public. This works, but is not ideal.

Either the "Auto-detect IMAP namespace" needs to be made more like Thunderbird desktop (able to detect multiple namespace paths) or there needs to be a syntax that allows a user to specify multiple paths. E.g., "/;Public" or something like that. Maybe such a syntax already exists? I couldn't find anything in the documentation about it.

Thanks for creating T-Bird Android. With the demise of MailDroid I have moved a bunch of users that I support over to T-Bird Android.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:139.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/139.0;os:Android 15

title: Starred emails
creator: kimberly16
content:
Hi Thunderbirds!

When I "Star" an email it disappears out of Inbox completely and I can only see it if I click on the "Star" option in the sorting menu. I've looked to see if there is a "Starred" folder but can't find one. I've now discovered that if someone replies to an email that I've starred I get no notification at all, it is just added to the thread. The next time I sort by Star I suddenly see unread replies to previously starred emails - bit of a nightmare! So, is there a way that I can adjust settings to: 1. Leave starred messages in the Inbox (or the folder that I move them to), but allow me to sort by Star when I need to? 2. Have new replies to previously starred messages appear as new notifications, the same way they do for normal emails.

Thanks! Kimberly

title: After installing Thunderbird Android to replace K9 mail I cannot send mails
creator: zaruba
content:
I have been running Tbird on my desktop for years, and it's still working great.

About 2 years ago I picked up a One Plus 11G which came with no e-mail client. I searched for Tbird and found K9 mail (with promises of Tbird soon), which I installed and used since then with no problems.

Few months ago, i got my wife a Samsung23+ and she used the supplied e-mail and messaging programs but constantly complained that she could have only one notification sound for both mails and texts and wanted to tell them apart. I told her I'd have to install a separate mail app so she could have two different sounds.

OK, I checked and finally found that Tbird was now available for Android so i installed it on her phone and she is very happy now.

Since it worked for her, I decided to install it on my phone to replace K9 Mail, and that's when the fun started.

It works great for incoming mails, but when I try to send mails via WiFi or via cell, I get a "Failed to send some messages sender refused" notification.

I got another phone and installed Tbird and it behaves the same way on that phone whether I set it up as POP (my usual setting) or IMAP.

I've installed other mail apps (GMAIL and Edison)and they send out with no problems both POP and IMAP.

If I go to the the Optonline web mail site via Firefox on my phone, I have no issues sending out mails, but that is a clunky way to do since Tbird normally worked so well as it is now or in iots earlier K9 version.

Any idea what is causing this problem only with Tbird? I really like Tbird and I don't like the other two apps.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:133.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/133.0
